tasks.description: Remove CLAUDE.md and update SCHEMA_DESIGN.md to describe training-sourced schema with deployment registry.

steps.description: Delete CLAUDE.md and ensure no references remain in repository docs.
Revise SCHEMA_DESIGN.md to explain schema flow, feature registry role, and functional composition pipeline.

tasks.acceptance-criteria: CLAUDE.md must be fully removed from the repo history and documentation. SCHEMA_DESIGN.md must state that schema.yaml originates from training, deployment loads feature_registry.yaml, and observation assembly relies on functional transform pipelines. Any diagrams or tables must reflect the new architecture.

e2e-manual: Manual review confirms SCHEMA_DESIGN.md accurately mirrors the agreed training/deployment contract and no files reference CLAUDE.md.

tasks.description: Define feature_registry.yaml schema describing transform pipelines, inputs, and runtime augmentations.

steps.description: Document YAML fields (version, features map, per-feature pipeline list, transform params, runtime flags).
List canonical transforms with their inputs and outputs to guide implementers.
Provide example registry entries for core features including target_error and gravity_projection.

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Written specification must describe registry structure precisely, including how each pipeline step names a registered transform and how inputs/contexts are provided. Examples must cover both static features and runtime-augmented ones. Spec must mention validation expectations (dimension, transform existence).

unit: YAML example validates successfully against documented schema using a schema validation tool or custom loader stub.

tasks.description: Build Python loader for feature_registry.yaml that manages transform registration, dynamic imports, and pipeline composition.

steps.description: Create dataclasses for FeatureSpecPipeline and TransformStep capturing pipeline metadata.
Implement loader that parses YAML, validates transform names against registry, and imports callable objects.
Expose FeatureRegistry API to fetch callable pipelines and list dependencies.
Write unit tests covering parse success, error handling for unknown transforms, and dimension mismatches.

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Loader must raise explicit errors when YAML is malformed, when requested transforms are missing, or when declared output dimensions do not match transform outputs. Registry API must allow retrieving a callable pipeline per feature that accepts context dict and produces numpy arrays. Tests must run via existing test harness.

unit: Parsing valid registry YAML yields FeatureSpecPipeline objects with correct attributes.
Unknown transform name triggers descriptive exception before pipeline creation.
Declared output_dim mismatching actual transform output raises validation error.
integration: Loader combined with actual transform functions from registry runs a sample feature pipeline end-to-end producing expected vectors.

tasks.description: Create ObservationSchema loader, SchemaValidator, and ObservationComposer that consume schema.yaml plus feature registry to emit observation vectors.

steps.description: Implement ObservationSchema dataclass with from_yaml, including total_dim reporting.
Implement SchemaValidator to check schema features exist in registry and match ONNX input dims.
Implement ObservationComposer that iterates schema order, invoking registry pipelines with provided context and appending runtime last_action.
Add unit tests for validator (success and failure cases) and composer (mock pipelines).

tasks.acceptance-criteria: ObservationSchema must read training-generated schema verbatim. Validator must report missing/extra features and dimension mismatches. Composer must accept context dicts, respect feature order, and append runtime features exactly once. Unit tests must simulate both valid and invalid scenarios.

unit: ObservationSchema.from_yaml loads schema_version, model_name, features, and computes total_dim accurately.
SchemaValidator rejects schemas with features absent from registry and reports clear errors.
ObservationComposer assembles observation vector correctly when provided mock pipelines returning known arrays.
integration: Composer plus real registry pipelines produce observation of expected dimension when fed synthetic PX4 context and last_action.
Validator confirms schema total_dim aligns with ONNX model input dimension via mocked ONNX metadata.

tasks.task: Integrate New Pipeline And Remove Adaptation Layer
tasks.description: Wire NeuralInferencePipeline to the new composer, delete legacy adaptation_config code, and adjust configs/tests accordingly.

steps.description: Update neural_infer pipeline initialization to load schema.yaml and feature_registry.yaml, invoking validator before runtime.
Replace adaptation pipeline usage with composer outputs inside inference loop, ensuring last_action handling matches training.
Remove unused adaptation_config/adaptors/transform registry files or refactor them into simple utility modules.
Update configuration files and tests to reference registry path and remove outdated settings.

tasks.acceptance-criteria: NeuralInferencePipeline must start successfully using new schema/registry inputs, and old adaptation modules must no longer be imported. Config files must expose registry path and no longer mention adaptation_config. Existing test suites must pass with the new flow, and new integration tests must cover composer-driven observation generation.

unit: Injection tests confirm pipeline raises informative errors when registry or schema files are missing.
Unit test for inference loop ensures composer outputs are concatenated with last_action before actor call.
integration: End-to-end simulated run with mock communicator feeds PX4 data through composer and verifies actor receives correctly shaped tensors.
Regression test confirms legacy adaptation modules are not imported during pipeline startup.
e2e-manual: Manual spin of ROS2 node in dry-run mode verifies logs show schema validation success and observation values change with mock inputs.
